subject: Social Science
overall_learning_objectives: Analyze water-related issues and global patterns
Understand the science behind water cycle, groundwater, and river systems
Evaluate causes and solutions of water scarcity and pollution
Develop research, presentation, and problem-solving skills
Encourage community awareness and advocacy for water conservation

lesson_plan.title: Global and National Water Resources
topics_covered: Major river basins in India and their significance
Comparison of water availability in different countries
teaching_strategy: Discussion of case studies on water-rich and water-scarce countries
student_activities: Locate major river basins on map
Compare water availability of India with other countries

lesson_plan.title: Detailed Water Cycle and Groundwater Dynamics
topics_covered: Detailed stages of the water cycle with energy considerations
Aquifers, recharge zones, and water table fluctuations
Effect of human activities on groundwater
teaching_strategy: Use diagrams and experiments to show evaporation, condensation, and recharge
Discuss the science behind groundwater depletion
student_activities: Draw labeled diagrams of aquifers and water cycle
Explain effects of overuse and pollution on groundwater

lesson_plan.title: Water Scarcity, Pollution, and Case Studies
topics_covered: Reasons and consequences of scarcity and pollution
Impact on health, agriculture, and ecosystems
Study of real-life cases like Yamuna pollution, droughts, and groundwater depletion
teaching_strategy: Show images, news reports, and data charts
student_activities: Analyze case studies and suggest solutions
Create a cause-effect chart of water issues

lesson_plan.title: Water Conservation Techniques
topics_covered: Advanced rainwater harvesting and watershed management
Micro-irrigation and efficient water use in agriculture
teaching_strategy: Discuss local and global water-saving projects
student_activities: Design a mini watershed or irrigation plan
Prepare a poster on water conservation methods

lesson_plan.title: Research and Problem Solving
topics_covered: Identify water problems in local area
Propose innovative solutions using learned methods
teaching_strategy: Guided research using local data and examples

lesson_plan.title: Review, Debate, and Quiz
topics_covered: Recap of water issues, conservation, and management
Debate on industrial vs. domestic water use
Quiz on facts, processes, and case studies
teaching_strategy: Class discussion for reflection and problem-solving

teaching_aids: Charts and diagrams of water cycle, aquifers, and irrigation
Videos on water scarcity and conservation
Materials for small projects or models
teacher_tip: Encourage analytical thinking and problem solving. Use local water examples. Include debates, projects, and case studies to connect theory with real-world issues.
